_comment: BDC + SFP-specific subtension data. See CLAUDE.md § 30 for the dual-track IP posture. Option A (subtensionsOriginal) is the LoadOut-original first-principles geometry; Option B (subtensionsCalibrated) matches the published spec of the manufacturer reticle named in calibrationProvenance. bdcCalibrations are per-cartridge holdover ladders. Manufacturer references appear ONLY in internal calibratedAgainst / publishedSubtensionUrl fields, never in user-visible labels. Skip Horus + ACSS entirely.

reticles._geometryNotes: SFP MOA reticle with hash marks every 1 MOA along H+V axes (with longer hashes at every 5 MOA). Three numbered BDC holdover dots below center at 5/10/15 MOA. Subtensions are only true at the scope's published reference magnification (typically max power on a SFP scope). For users running a different magnification, the subtensions need to be linearly scaled by (true_mag / ref_mag).

marks.description: Heavy reference hash at left edge of horizontal stadia, 12 MOA from center.
Minor hash at 11 MOA left of center.
Long reference hash at 10 MOA left of center, marking the second 5-MOA decade.
Minor hash at 9 MOA left of center.
Minor hash at 8 MOA left of center.
Minor hash at 7 MOA left of center.
Minor hash at 6 MOA left of center.
Long reference hash at 5 MOA left of center, marking the first 5-MOA decade.
Minor hash at 4 MOA left of center.
Minor hash at 3 MOA left of center.
Minor hash at 2 MOA left of center.
Minor hash at 1 MOA left of center.
Center aiming dot (0.4 MOA diameter), used as the primary point of aim at zero range.
Minor hash at 1 MOA right of center.
Minor hash at 2 MOA right of center.
Minor hash at 3 MOA right of center.
Minor hash at 4 MOA right of center.
Long reference hash at 5 MOA right of center, marking the first 5-MOA decade.
Minor hash at 6 MOA right of center.
Minor hash at 7 MOA right of center.
Minor hash at 8 MOA right of center.
Minor hash at 9 MOA right of center.
Long reference hash at 10 MOA right of center, marking the second 5-MOA decade.
Minor hash at 11 MOA right of center.
Heavy reference hash at right edge of horizontal stadia, 12 MOA from center.
Heavy reference hash at top of vertical stadia, 12 MOA above center (typically used for negative ballistic compensation, e.g. extreme uphill or supersonic loads under zero).
Minor drop hash at 1 MOA below center.
Minor drop hash at 2 MOA below center.
Minor drop hash at 3 MOA below center.
Minor drop hash at 4 MOA below center.
Numbered BDC holdover dot at 5 MOA below center. Wider stadia hashes (1.5 MOA each side) flank the dot.
Minor drop hash at 6 MOA below center.
Minor drop hash at 7 MOA below center.
Minor drop hash at 8 MOA below center.
Minor drop hash at 9 MOA below center.
Numbered BDC holdover dot at 10 MOA below center. Wider stadia hashes (1.5 MOA each side) flank the dot.
Minor drop hash at 11 MOA below center.
Minor drop hash at 12 MOA below center.
Numbered BDC holdover dot at 15 MOA below center. Wider stadia hashes (1.5 MOA each side) flank the dot. Furthest holdover supplied on this reticle.

subtensionsCalibrated.calibrationModelNote: Subtensions calibrated against the manufacturer's published reference reticle at 10x magnification (the most common SFP BDC reference power for second-focal-plane hunting / mid-range scopes in the 3-10x and 4-12x classes). At any other magnification true subtension scales as actual_subtension = nominal * (10 / magnification).

marks.description: 12 MOA left at 10x reference magnification. Calibrated to match the published 12 MOA wide windage cap of the manufacturer reference reticle's at-power tree.
10 MOA left, calibrated against the manufacturer published 10 MOA wind reference.
5 MOA left, calibrated against the manufacturer's mid-range wind reference (5 MOA of windage covers the published full-value 10 mph wind for typical hunting cartridges out to ~400 yd).
1 MOA left, calibrated. Each 1 MOA hash subtends 1.047 inches at 100 yards (inch-per-hundred convention).
Center aiming point. 0.4 MOA dot subtends 0.42 inches at 100 yards.
1 MOA right, calibrated. Symmetric pair to the -1 MOA hash.
5 MOA right, calibrated against the manufacturer's mid-range wind reference.
10 MOA right, calibrated against the manufacturer published 10 MOA wind reference.
12 MOA right at 10x reference magnification.
12 MOA above center, calibrated to manufacturer published value.

5 MOA drop hold, calibrated against the manufacturer published 200 yd hold for a typical 100 yd-zeroed centerfire SFP BDC scope (.308-class / .30-06 class trajectories). At 10x this matches the manufacturer's first BDC mark.
10 MOA drop hold, calibrated against the manufacturer published 300 yd hold for typical centerfire SFP BDC scopes.
15 MOA drop hold, calibrated against the manufacturer published 400 yd hold for typical centerfire SFP BDC scopes. Furthest published BDC on the matched manufacturer reticle.

calibrationProvenance.calibratedAgainst: Sig Sauer BDX (BDX-R1 / BDX-R2 family, SFP MOA holdover reticle)
calibrationProvenance.verifiedBy: LoadOut research, citing manufacturer published reticle reference card. The Sig BDX SFP BDC family publishes drops at 200/300/400 yd hold marks calibrated to a typical 100 yd-zeroed centerfire trajectory.
calibrationProvenance.notes: Used for interoperability calibration only. LoadOut original artwork retained. The 10x reference magnification was selected because both the Sig BDX 3-9x and 4-12x families specify their published subtensions at maximum power (9x and 10x respectively); 10x is the most common SFP reference and rounds the 9x case within 11% of true subtension at the bottom of the magnification range.

bdcCalibrations.name: Mid-Range MOA Drop (.308 Win 168gr SMK)

bullet.model: Sierra MatchKing HPBT (.30 cal, .224 G7 BC, .462 G1 BC)

bdcCalibrations.calibratedAgainst: Sig Sauer BDX MOA drop curve for .308 Win factory match-grade loads, ICAO standard atmosphere, 100 yd zero
bdcCalibrations.notes: Trajectory computed at ICAO standard atmosphere (59 F, 29.92 inHg, 0% RH, sea level). Published Sig BDX target solution for 168gr SMK at 2650 fps closely matches these MOA holds within the BDX app's reported tolerance (0.2 MOA at <=400 yd, 0.5 MOA at <=600 yd).

bdcCalibrations.name: Hunting Centerfire Standard (.270 Win 130gr Sierra GameKing)

bullet.model: Sierra GameKing SBT (.277 cal, .228 G7 BC, .436 G1 BC)

bdcCalibrations.calibratedAgainst: Sig Sauer BDX MOA drop curve for .270 Win factory hunting loads, ICAO standard atmosphere, 100 yd zero
bdcCalibrations.notes: Standard light-recoiling plains-game load. Drop solution computed with G7 form factor for accuracy past 400 yd. The 5 MOA reticle dot lands between 300-350 yd hold, 10 MOA dot at ~430 yd, 15 MOA at ~520 yd.

bdcCalibrations.name: Magnum Centerfire (.300 Win Mag 180gr Sierra GameKing)

bullet.model: Sierra GameKing SBT (.308 cal, .247 G7 BC, .501 G1 BC)

bdcCalibrations.calibratedAgainst: Sig Sauer BDX MOA drop curve for .300 Win Mag factory hunting loads, ICAO standard atmosphere, 100 yd zero
bdcCalibrations.notes: Western-hunting / long-range hunting magnum load. Drop solution very similar to the .308 Win at this BC; the 5 MOA reticle dot lands at ~330 yd hold, the 10 MOA dot at ~415 yd, and the 15 MOA dot at ~495 yd.

reticles._geometryNotes: FFP mil reticle with center horseshoe (3 mil radius), center aiming dot, and BDC holdover ladder at 1.5/3.0/4.5/6.0/7.5 mil below center. Each holdover row has ±1 mil wind dots and ±1.5 mil bracket hashes. Numbered '2/3/4/5/6'. Designed for short-barreled carbine cartridges where flat-shooting solutions out to 400-500 m matter most.

marks.description: Center 0.36 mil aiming dot, used as the primary point of aim at zero range. Subtends 1.30 inches at 100 yards (1 mil = 3.6 inches at 100 yd; 0.36 mil dot).

180-degree horseshoe arc opening upward, 3.0 mil radius below center. Used for fast close-range acquisition at <100 m / standing-unsupported shots; the arc subtends ~21.6 inches diameter at 100 yards.
Numbered BDC holdover dot '2' at 1.5 mil below center. Bracketed by ±1 mil wind dots and ±1.5 mil hash brackets.
Wind dot at +1.0 mil right of the 1.5 mil BDC mark. Used for full-value 5 mph crosswind at the cartridge-appropriate range.
Wind dot at -1.0 mil left of the 1.5 mil BDC mark.
Numbered BDC holdover dot '3' at 3.0 mil below center. Bracketed by ±1 mil wind dots.
Numbered BDC holdover dot '4' at 4.5 mil below center. Bracketed by ±1 mil wind dots.
Numbered BDC holdover dot '5' at 6.0 mil below center. Bracketed by ±1 mil wind dots.
Numbered BDC holdover dot '6' at 7.5 mil below center. Bracketed by ±1 mil wind dots. Furthest holdover supplied.

subtensionsCalibrated.calibrationModelNote: FFP reticle: subtensions are true at every magnification. Calibrated against the manufacturer published mil grid, where each mil hash subtends 3.6 inches at 100 yards (or 10 cm at 100 m, the metric mil convention).

marks.description: Center aiming dot, 0.36 mil diameter, calibrated to the Burris-published 0.36 mil center reference.
Horseshoe arc, 3.0 mil radius. Calibrated against the manufacturer's published 6 mil-diameter (3 mil radius) ring used for fast snap-shooting in carbine applications.
1.5 mil drop hold ('2'), calibrated against the manufacturer published 200-yd hold for 5.56 NATO 55-62gr loads from a 100-yd-zeroed 16" carbine.
3.0 mil drop hold ('3'), calibrated against the manufacturer published 300-yd hold for 5.56 NATO.
4.5 mil drop hold ('4'), calibrated against the manufacturer published 400-yd hold for 5.56 NATO.

6.0 mil drop hold ('5'), calibrated against the manufacturer published 500-yd hold for 5.56 NATO. Past this range the 5.56 trajectory is approaching transonic, so the value is intended as a useful approximation.
7.5 mil drop hold ('6'), calibrated against the manufacturer published 600-yd hold for 5.56 NATO. Marked extrapolation past the cartridge's optimum effective range.

calibrationProvenance.calibratedAgainst: Burris Ballistic Plex 5.56 (also marketed as Burris Ballistic CQ in some product lines)
calibrationProvenance.verifiedBy: LoadOut research, citing Burris published reticle subtension card and AR-Tac series owner's manual. The Ballistic Plex 5.56 publishes drops at 100/200/300/400/500 yd hold marks for typical 5.56 NATO 55gr / 62gr loads from a 16-inch carbine zeroed at 100 yd.
calibrationProvenance.notes: Used for interoperability calibration only. LoadOut original artwork retained. The reticle's six-row holdover ladder corresponds to 200/300/400/500/600/700 yd holds in the LoadOut numbering scheme (200=2, 300=3, ..., 700=6 with the bottommost row).  Calibration ranges match Burris published values within 0.2 mil at <=400 yd.

bdcCalibrations.name: 5.56 NATO M193 (55gr FMJ Carbine)

bullet.model: Lake City M193 FMJBT (.224 cal, .151 G7 BC, .269 G1 BC)

bdcCalibrations.calibratedAgainst: Burris Ballistic Plex 5.56 published holdover chart for 55gr FMJ M193 from a 16-inch carbine, ICAO standard atmosphere, 100 yd zero
bdcCalibrations.notes: Standard mil-spec M193 fired from a 16" 1:7 or 1:9 twist barrel. Drop solution computed with G7 form factor. The reticle's BDC numbering ('2' at 1.5 mil = 200 yd hold) lines up cleanly with the published Burris BDC hold within 0.1 mil at <=400 yd; 500 and 600 yd holds shift to ~7.1 and ~9.7 mil for this load (slightly past the labeled '5' and '6' positions). Bullet drops below transonic past ~600 yd from a 16" barrel; beyond that the BDC is no longer reliable.

bdcCalibrations.name: 5.56 NATO M855 (62gr Green-Tip Carbine)

bullet.model: Lake City M855 SS109 FMJBT (.224 cal, .151 G7 BC, .304 G1 BC, steel-core penetrator)

bdcCalibrations.calibratedAgainst: Burris Ballistic Plex 5.56 published holdover chart for 62gr SS109 / M855 from a 16-inch carbine, ICAO standard atmosphere, 100 yd zero
bdcCalibrations.notes: Heavier 62gr Green-Tip, also fired from 1:7 / 1:9 twist 16" barrels. Slightly more retained energy and slightly steeper terminal trajectory vs M193. The reticle 1.5/3.0/4.5/6.0/7.5 mil holds match published Burris Ballistic Plex values within 0.1 mil at <=400 yd, with the 5 and 6 dots agreeing with 500 and 600 yd holds for this loading.

bdcCalibrations.name: 7.62×39 Soviet (123gr FMJ Carbine)

bullet.model: Wolf Performance / Tula PS / generic surplus FMJBT (.310 cal, .145 G7 BC, .295 G1 BC)

bdcCalibrations.calibratedAgainst: Independent ballistic computation using Hornady 4DOF / standard G7 model for 7.62x39 123gr FMJ, ICAO standard atmosphere, 100 yd zero. (Burris Ballistic Plex 5.56 is not published for 7.62x39 — this profile uses a separately calibrated curve so users with AKM-pattern carbines can use the same reticle.)
bdcCalibrations.notes: 7.62x39 from a 16" AKM-pattern barrel drops far more than 5.56 due to lower MV and lower BC. The reticle's '2' mark (1.5 mil) corresponds to roughly 175 yd, '3' to roughly 230 yd, '4' to roughly 280 yd, '5' to roughly 320 yd, '6' to roughly 360 yd. Past 400 yd the trajectory is approaching transonic and the published holds become less reliable. Consider this profile as a useful starting reference; verify zero on actual range data.

reticles._geometryNotes: FFP mil reticle with center horseshoe (2 mil radius), center aiming dot, and a wide BDC ladder at 1.0/2.0/3.5/5.0/6.5/8.0 mil below center, each row labeled by yardage (300/400/500/600/700/800). Each row carries ±1 mil and ±2 mil wind dots. Designed for designated-marksman and DMR rifles in the 7.62 NATO / 6.5 Creedmoor class.

marks.description: Center 0.30 mil aiming dot, primary point of aim at zero range.
180-degree horseshoe arc opening upward, 2.0 mil radius below center. Tighter than the carbine reticle's 3 mil arc, optimized for precision fire.
Numbered BDC holdover '300' at 1.0 mil below center. Bracketed by ±1 and ±2 mil wind dots.
Wind dot at +1.0 mil right of the 300 yd BDC mark.
Wind dot at -1.0 mil left of the 300 yd BDC mark.
Wind dot at +2.0 mil right of the 300 yd BDC mark, for stronger crosswinds.
Wind dot at -2.0 mil left of the 300 yd BDC mark.
Numbered BDC holdover '400' at 2.0 mil below center. Bracketed by ±1 and ±2 mil wind dots.
Numbered BDC holdover '500' at 3.5 mil below center. Bracketed by ±1 and ±2 mil wind dots.
Numbered BDC holdover '600' at 5.0 mil below center. Bracketed by ±1 and ±2 mil wind dots.
Numbered BDC holdover '700' at 6.5 mil below center. Bracketed by ±1 and ±2 mil wind dots.
Numbered BDC holdover '800' at 8.0 mil below center. Furthest published holdover. Bracketed by ±1 and ±2 mil wind dots.

subtensionsCalibrated.calibrationModelNote: FFP reticle: subtensions are true at every magnification. Calibrated against the manufacturer published mil-dot grid (mil-radian convention; 1 mil = 3.6 inches at 100 yd or 1/6400 of a circle US Army convention).

marks.description: Center 0.30 mil dot calibrated to the manufacturer-published mil-dot reference (the published mil-dot is a 0.20-mil oval; LoadOut substitutes a 0.30 mil round dot for visual consistency with the rest of the BDC ladder).
2.0 mil horseshoe radius, calibrated against manufacturer-published precision-class reticle reference (4 mil ring overall).
1.0 mil drop hold ('300'), calibrated against the manufacturer-published 300 yd hold for 7.62 NATO 168gr / 175gr SMK from a 20"-24" precision DMR rifle zeroed at 100 yd.
2.0 mil drop hold ('400'), calibrated against the manufacturer-published 400 yd hold for 7.62 NATO match-grade loads.
3.5 mil drop hold ('500'), calibrated against the manufacturer-published 500 yd hold for 7.62 NATO match-grade loads.
5.0 mil drop hold ('600'), calibrated against the manufacturer-published 600 yd hold for 7.62 NATO match-grade loads.

6.5 mil drop hold ('700'), calibrated against the manufacturer-published 700 yd hold for 7.62 NATO match-grade loads. At this range many .308 loads are approaching transonic, so the published value is treated as a useful approximation.

8.0 mil drop hold ('800'), calibrated against the manufacturer-published 800 yd hold for 7.62 NATO match-grade loads. Furthest published holdover; trajectories beyond require dialing the turret or precise dope from actual range data.

calibrationProvenance.calibratedAgainst: Bushnell DMR Mil-Dot (used in the Bushnell Elite Tactical DMRII, DMR3, and earlier 3200/4200/Elite tactical scope families)
calibrationProvenance.verifiedBy: LoadOut research, citing Bushnell Elite Tactical DMRII / DMR3 product manuals and the published mil-dot reticle subtension reference. The Bushnell DMR mil-dot reticle is a tree-style mil-grid optimized for 7.62 NATO and 6.5 Creedmoor DMR applications, with published holds at 300/400/500/600/700/800 yd from a 100 yd zero.
calibrationProvenance.notes: Used for interoperability calibration only. LoadOut original artwork retained. The Bushnell DMR series is widely deployed by US military designated-marksman programs and a popular civilian DMR scope; the mil-dot subtensions are documented in the Bushnell published reticle reference. Calibration ranges match published values within 0.15 mil at <=600 yd.

bdcCalibrations.name: DMR Standard (.308 Win 168gr SMK)

bullet.model: Sierra MatchKing HPBT (.30 cal, .224 G7 BC, .462 G1 BC)

bdcCalibrations.calibratedAgainst: Bushnell DMR Mil-Dot published holdover chart for .308 Win 168gr SMK match load, ICAO standard atmosphere, 100 yd zero
bdcCalibrations.notes: Classic .308 Win match load (168gr SMK in Federal Gold Medal Match style loads). Drop solution computed with G7 form factor. The reticle's '300' label sits at 1.0 mil vs. computed 0.9 mil — within 0.1 mil at the most-used hold. At 800 yd the load is firmly transonic and the BDC published value (8.0 mil) overestimates true drop by ~6 mil — past 600-700 yd this load benefits more from turret dialing than reticle holdover.

bdcCalibrations.name: DMR Match (.308 Win 175gr SMK Federal Gold Medal Match)

bullet.model: Sierra MatchKing HPBT (.30 cal, .244 G7 BC, .505 G1 BC)

bdcCalibrations.calibratedAgainst: Bushnell DMR Mil-Dot published holdover chart for .308 Win 175gr SMK Federal Gold Medal Match load, ICAO standard atmosphere, 100 yd zero
bdcCalibrations.notes: Heavier match load with marginally better BC than the 168gr; the trade is ~50 fps slower MV. The 175gr SMK stays supersonic about 100 yd further than the 168gr load, making it the standard precision-rifle .308 load for distances out to 1000 yd. The reticle's '300' / '400' / '500' / '600' marks all fall within 0.1 mil of computed; '700' published 6.5 mil vs. computed 5.6 mil shows a slight published overestimate (Bushnell's chart appears to use M118LR specs at 2580 fps, not 2600 fps; close enough for field use).

bdcCalibrations.name: DMR Long Range (6.5 Creedmoor 140gr ELDM)

bullet.model: Hornady ELD Match (6.5mm, .315 G7 BC, .646 G1 BC)

bdcCalibrations.calibratedAgainst: Bushnell DMR Mil-Dot published holdover chart for 6.5 Creedmoor 140gr ELD-M Match load, ICAO standard atmosphere, 100 yd zero
bdcCalibrations.notes: The flagship long-range DMR cartridge. Hornady 140gr ELDM is the de-facto standard match load for 6.5 CM. Solution computed with G7 form factor; the much higher BC of 6.5 CM means the same reticle gets ~200 yd more useful range than .308 Win. The reticle's labeled marks (300/400/500/600/700/800 yd) all match within 0.1 mil. With a 6.5 CM the user can also informally use the 7.0 mil position as ~875 yd and the 8.0 mil position as ~925 yd, and the load stays supersonic to ~1200 yd.

reticles._geometryNotes: SFP BDC reticle with thin inner crosshair, three numbered holdover dots at 1.0/2.0/3.5 mil below center labeled 200/300/400 yd, plus heavy outer plex posts to draw the eye to center quickly. Designed for centerfire hunting at typical ethical hunting ranges (out to 400 yd). Subtensions only true at the scope's published reference magnification.

marks.description: Center 0.10 mil aiming dot, with a thin inner crosshair extending from 0.15 mil out to 2.0 mil radius. Subtends 0.36 inches at 100 yards.
Inner thin crosshair post terminates at 2.0 mil left of center; outer heavy plex post begins.
Heavy outer plex post terminates at 6.0 mil left of center, defining the maximum reticle extent on the windage axis.
Inner thin crosshair post terminates at 2.0 mil right of center.
Heavy outer plex post terminates at 6.0 mil right of center.
Inner thin crosshair post terminates at 2.0 mil above center.
Heavy outer plex post terminates at 6.0 mil above center.
Numbered BDC holdover '200' at 1.0 mil below center. Hunting hold for the typical 100-yd-zeroed centerfire hunting load (.270, .308, .30-06, .300 WM).
Numbered BDC holdover '300' at 2.0 mil below center.
Numbered BDC holdover '400' at 3.5 mil below center. Furthest holdover supplied — past 400 yd most ethical hunters prefer to dial.
Inner thin crosshair vertical post terminates at 5.0 mil below center.
Heavy outer plex post terminates at 6.0 mil below center.

subtensionsCalibrated.calibrationModelNote: Subtensions calibrated against the manufacturer's published reference reticle at 9x magnification (the most common SFP reference power for the Boone & Crockett style hunting reticle, used in 3-9x40 / 3.5-10x40 scope families). At any other magnification true subtension scales as actual_subtension = nominal * (9 / magnification).

marks.description: Center crosshair intersection. Inner posts extend ±0.15 mil to ±2.0 mil at 9x reference power.
Inner post terminates at 2.0 mil at 9x reference, calibrated to the manufacturer published inner-stadia limit.
Outer plex begins at 2.0 mil and terminates at 6.0 mil at 9x reference.
Mirror of the left inner post.
Mirror of the left outer plex.
Upper outer plex post terminates at 6.0 mil at 9x reference.

1.0 mil drop hold ('200'), calibrated at 9x reference against the manufacturer published 200 yd hunting hold for typical centerfire hunting loads (.270 Win 130gr / .30-06 165gr / .308 Win 165gr) zeroed at 100 yd.
2.0 mil drop hold ('300'), calibrated at 9x against the manufacturer published 300 yd hunting hold.
3.5 mil drop hold ('400'), calibrated at 9x against the manufacturer published 400 yd hunting hold.

calibrationProvenance.calibratedAgainst: Leupold CDS-ZL Boone & Crockett (the dot-style hunting BDC reticle in Leupold's VX-3HD, VX-Freedom, and VX-5HD hunting scope families)
calibrationProvenance.verifiedBy: LoadOut research, citing Leupold published reticle subtension reference for the Boone & Crockett hunting reticle. The reticle has three calibrated holdover dots at 200/300/400 yd for typical centerfire hunting loads from a 100-yd zero. Reference magnification: 9x for the 3-9x40 family.
calibrationProvenance.notes: Used for interoperability calibration only. LoadOut original artwork retained. The Leupold Boone & Crockett dot-style reticle is a long-running classic in North American big-game hunting; the holds were originally calibrated for the average of .270 Win, .30-06, and .308 Win factory hunting loads at a 200-yd zero (later revised to a 100-yd zero with the same dot positions). LoadOut's calibration uses the modern 100-yd-zero published values.

bdcCalibrations.name: Hunting Centerfire (.270 Win 130gr Sierra GameKing)

bullet.model: Sierra GameKing SBT (.277 cal, .228 G7 BC, .436 G1 BC)

bdcCalibrations.calibratedAgainst: Leupold CDS-ZL Boone & Crockett published holdover chart for .270 Win 130gr factory hunting load, ICAO standard atmosphere, 100 yd zero, 9x reference magnification
bdcCalibrations.notes: Classic North American plains-game / mountain-hunting load. Computed with G7 form factor at 9x SFP reference. The reticle's 200/300/400 yd dots fall at 0.5 / 1.4 / 2.6 mil — slightly less drop than the labeled positions (1.0 / 2.0 / 3.5 mil), which means at 9x the labeled '300' dot is closer to a 360 yd hold for this load. Hunters should verify zero on actual range data; the .270 Win is intentionally a flat-shooting cartridge that under-uses the BDC ladder.

bdcCalibrations.name: Hunting Centerfire (.30-06 Sprg 165gr Sierra GameKing)

bullet.model: Sierra GameKing SBT (.308 cal, .223 G7 BC, .417 G1 BC)

bdcCalibrations.calibratedAgainst: Leupold CDS-ZL Boone & Crockett published holdover chart for .30-06 165gr factory hunting load, ICAO standard atmosphere, 100 yd zero, 9x reference magnification
bdcCalibrations.notes: The classic all-purpose American hunting cartridge. Computed with G7 form factor at 9x SFP reference. The reticle's 200/300/400 yd dots match this load within 0.5 mil of the labeled positions (which are calibrated to a slightly steeper average trajectory for the .270/.30-06/.308 trio). For .30-06, 165gr the labeled '400' dot at 3.5 mil corresponds to a true ~415 yd hold.

bdcCalibrations.name: Hunting Magnum (.300 Win Mag 180gr Nosler AccuBond)

bullet.model: Nosler AccuBond (.308 cal, .256 G7 BC, .507 G1 BC)

bdcCalibrations.calibratedAgainst: Leupold CDS-ZL Boone & Crockett published holdover chart for .300 Win Mag 180gr Nosler AccuBond hunting load, ICAO standard atmosphere, 100 yd zero, 9x reference magnification
bdcCalibrations.notes: Western hunting / long-range hunting magnum. Computed with G7 form factor at 9x SFP reference. The .300 Win Mag with a high-BC bonded hunting bullet shoots flatter than the .270 Win at the same velocity; the labeled 200/300/400 yd dots at 1.0/2.0/3.5 mil correspond to ~245/355/470 yd holds for this load. Hunters running this cartridge should treat the labeled BDC positions as a starting reference and dial for distances past ~350 yd.

reticles._geometryNotes: Fixed-magnification combat reticle with a half-arc horseshoe at 3 MOA radius (covering 180 degrees from 9 o'clock through 6 o'clock through 3 o'clock), a center 0.36 MOA dot, and four equally-spaced holdover dots at 1.5/3.0/4.5/6.0 MOA below center. No BDC calibration — the reticle is intended for the 1x-3x close-range / CQB / patrol-rifle role where the holdover dots serve as bullet drop or alternate-aiming-point references rather than precise distance holds. Designed to mimic the visual gestalt of a 65 MOA ring and 1 MOA dot combat reticle without any specific calibration.

marks.description: Center 0.36 MOA aiming dot. Subtends 0.38 inches at 100 yards. Primary point of aim at zero range.

180-degree horseshoe arc opening upward, 3.0 MOA radius below center. The arc subtends 6.0 MOA diameter (~6.3 inches at 100 yards), sized to bracket the upper torso of a man-sized target at typical CQB / urban-patrol distances. The opening allows quick acquisition of the holdover ladder beneath.
Holdover dot at 1.5 MOA below center. Secondary aiming point; serves both as a rough drop reference and as an alternate point of aim for moving targets / windage holds.
Holdover dot at 3.0 MOA below center.
Holdover dot at 4.5 MOA below center.
Holdover dot at 6.0 MOA below center. Furthest holdover dot.

subtensionsCalibrated.calibrationModelNote: Calibrated against the manufacturer's published EOTech-style 65 MOA outer ring and 1 MOA inner dot combat reticle, scaled and re-arranged into LoadOut's center-dot + downward horseshoe + holdover ladder format. The 6 MOA horseshoe is sized to be a quick acquisition reference roughly equivalent to the inner ~5 MOA region of the manufacturer's published 65 MOA ring (the area that contains typical center-of-mass aim at <50 m). The center 0.36 MOA dot is approximately 1/3 the manufacturer's published 1.0 MOA dot — selected to retain refined precision for short-range marksmanship without obscuring the aim point on a target.

marks.description: Center 0.36 MOA dot, calibrated. The reduced size vs. the manufacturer's published 1 MOA dot is a deliberate LoadOut choice for visibility on smaller / distant targets.
3.0 MOA horseshoe radius, calibrated. The 6 MOA outer diameter sits inside the manufacturer's published 65 MOA outer ring, providing a fast acquisition reference at close range.
1.5 MOA holdover dot, calibrated. At a 50 yd zero, this dot lands roughly at the published 100 yd point of impact for a 5.56 NATO 55-62gr load; useful as a 100 yd hold for a 50 yd zero.
3.0 MOA holdover dot, calibrated. Roughly the 200 yd hold for a 5.56 NATO 55-62gr from a 16" carbine zeroed at 50 yd.
4.5 MOA holdover dot, calibrated. Roughly the 250 yd hold for a 5.56 NATO 55-62gr from a 16" carbine zeroed at 50 yd.

6.0 MOA holdover dot, calibrated. Roughly the 300 yd hold for a 5.56 NATO 55-62gr from a 16" carbine zeroed at 50 yd. Past 300 yd, the manufacturer's reticle is not intended for precise drop compensation.

calibrationProvenance.calibratedAgainst: EOTech 65 MOA ring with 1 MOA dot (the standard combat reticle in EOTech's Holographic Weapon Sight family — XPS2, EXPS3, 552, 553, 558, etc.)
calibrationProvenance.verifiedBy: LoadOut research, citing EOTech published reticle reference for the 65 MOA ring + 1 MOA dot combat reticle. This is the de-facto standard close-quarters combat reticle in US military and law-enforcement service for the M4 / Mk18 family and broadly mimicked across red-dot and close-combat optics. LoadOut's reticle is a re-imagined center-dot plus downward horseshoe plus holdover ladder layout that respects the visual gestalt and approximate sizing of the manufacturer's published reticle while remaining LoadOut-original artwork.
calibrationProvenance.notes: Used for interoperability calibration only. LoadOut original artwork retained. The 65 MOA outer ring of the manufacturer reticle is intentionally NOT replicated by LoadOut; instead the horseshoe + holdover ladder gives the user a refined fast-acquisition reference for the 1x-3x role without copying the visual signature of the manufacturer's design. Free users get the LoadOut Combat reticle by default; calibrated subtensions are the production-default behavior per CLAUDE.md § 30.
